summary: Gardner syndrome includes multiple osteomas and an elevated risk for developing osteosarcoma, making these conditions more consistent than epidermoid cysts or impacted permanent teeth which may be present but aren't defining markers specific enough to exclude based solely on their presence versus absence within the context of diagnosing Gardner syndrome accurately. Option C, therefore, correctly identifies something outside its usual presentation. To clarify further regarding potential confusion around osteoma vs sarcoma association—osteomas themselves characterize GS whereas sarcomatous changes represent complications rather than primary findings indicative across every case definitively ruling out inclusion here under standard diagnostic parameters set forth by current understanding of Gardner’s condition., thus emphasizing why
